channel.title: Comments for page "May The Sun Never Set On The British Empire"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "May The Sun Never Set On The British Empire"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: a text-based RPG https://www.fallenlondon.com
I do like myself some additions to When Day Breaks, and this one is no exception.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let us read the tale of how the Sun challenged the British Empire. And how the sun lost.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this makes me wonder if the other city that was mentioned exists.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My god, that intercepted radio signal hit me hard. Just…ugh. Take my +1

item.title: Marvelous
item.content:encoded:
When I wrote Three Thousand to One, I was hoping that more people would pick up the thread of "Hey what about the people who would Survive DayBreaks?", and quite frankly I couldn't pick a better team to do that writing!

Look forwards to anyone else picking up the thread as well!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fallen Londeez nutz

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: scpillu i didn't know you could write…

item.title: I love this.
item.content:encoded:
I just can't tell you how much this tale has impacted me. A chance encounter by youtube recommendation led me to discover this combination of two inherently interesting SCPs and the skill of which it was written… its just amazing.

And when I a few months ago heard "I cast you into perdition's flame!" I decided then that I would join the SCP wiki, and one day get my own Scp up. Now, it took me about six months to get the first thing,

But now that I have actually gotten to it, I have never before felt so completely inspired in a creative endeavor.

As I said, I love this. Keep it up. Please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was rereading when day breaks, because I enjoy it quite a lot, and when I saw this crosslink I thought to myself… wait a minute. this must be a somewhat recent addition, I don't remember this from before? turns out I was right!

I hope to see more of this canon in the future. this was a great start. I like the idea that this is the reason for unlondon, I loved the part where the bobbies made room for thr researchers to get through. and I love the idea that we might get to meet the creator of unlondon, considering some of those announcement messages.

item.title: Wait wait wait
item.content:encoded:
Does this mean that there is a "Un Earth", and a "Un Universe?"

Moreover, is there "UnWashington", "UnBerlin", and such? An UnFoundation?

item.title: I found this to be fantastic.
item.content:encoded: This really made it feel like this is what UnLondon was for, for when day breaks. With the Bobbies stopping their attacks and the pigeons trying to warn researchers, it feels like they knew it was coming, and had the genuine intelligence to help the researchers. This really is the foundation now, and from there, all there is to do is to rebuild.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it's good that we know what happened to site 19 that everyone is dead and that no place is safe but i see multiple sites in the video still on lockdown i wonder if those sites are gonna get a story

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what's Fallen London

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Had to read UnLondon to get what it was about, but very cool. +1, would love to read a part 2 with UnDublin
The recording on SCP illustrated gave me shivers, your prose is absolutely on point!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 from me, but personally i would put the youtube video under the info bar. there's also some proofreading naggles (uncapitalized "foundation" in a few spots for example). still very cool to see though!

item.title: Watched the video from SCP illustrated, one of the best tails I read
item.content:encoded: So I watched the video from SCP illustrated, and just got a notification that it was now a tale. I really loved this, and would like to see what happens when they reached Undublin.

item.title: Outstanding!
item.content:encoded:
SCP Illustrated brought me here and I have to say, this was a fantastic tale. It combined two of my favorite SCP’s into a relatively short but harrowing story that kept me enthralled throughout.

The idea that there are more underground safe havens in addition to UnLondon has a great amount of potential, and I can’t wait to see where you guys go in terms of exploring UnDublin. I’m also looking forward to reading the tales involving survivors utilizing other SCP’s to escape from the sun’s wrath.

Great job to everyone involved, and I can’t wait to see what else is in store for this storyline! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Big thanks to Dr Cimmerian, he did a great job of writing up this little idea of mine. Looking forward to making more in the When Day Breaks storyline with him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Coauthor with myself and SCP Illustrated. Involves When Day Breaks and Unlondon and is pretty heavily influenced by Fallen London, obviously.

Hope you enjoyed it.
